description: Domain vocabulary and term definitions for the CourtListener REST API, covering US federal and state court legal data.

terms.definition: A court opinion or decision document. May be a combined opinion, majority, concurrence, dissent, or other opinion type.
A group of related opinions arising from a single case event (e.g., majority + concurrence + dissent from same ruling).
A PACER docket record representing a case in a US federal or state court. Contains the list of docket entries and parties.
A single entry within a court docket, representing a filing, order, or other case event.
A PACER document retrieved via the RECAP (Reverse PACER) project, stored and freely accessible on CourtListener.
An oral argument audio recording from a federal appellate court, stored as an MP3 file.
A US court record, including federal district courts, circuit courts of appeals, the Supreme Court, and state courts.
A judge, attorney, or other legal professional tracked in the CourtListener database.
A judicial or legal position held by a person, such as a district judge appointment.
Annual financial disclosure report filed by a federal judge as required by law.
A saved search alert that notifies the user when new results matching their query are published.
An alert for a specific docket that notifies the user when new filings appear.
A citation network visualization showing the relationship between cases.
Public Access to Court Electronic Records — the US federal courts' electronic filing and retrieval system.
A project that archives and freely distributes PACER documents. RECAP is PACER spelled backwards.
A reference from one court opinion to another, forming a citation network. CourtListener tracks 500M+ citations.
The number of times a court opinion has been cited by other opinions in the CourtListener database.
Flag indicating whether a document is blocked from appearing in search engine results (e.g., to protect privacy).
A numeric code (from PACER) indicating the nature of the civil suit, such as '440' for Other Civil Rights.
The unique case identifier used in the PACER system for a given federal court case.
The canonical URI for a CourtListener API resource, used to retrieve or reference the resource.
The relative URL path to the human-readable page for a resource on CourtListener.com.
The geographic or governmental scope of a court's authority (e.g., federal, state, territory).
The type or role of a court opinion within a case decision.
The Federal Judicial Center unique identifier for a federal judge.
An opaque string used for cursor-based pagination in list endpoints, replacing page-number pagination.
A free API access tier for academic/educational users, available at no cost with .edu email verification.
